NORTHROOT
ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples

Section

ProductSystemsDocsJournalExamples
Product

Kernel Boundary

What Northroot is responsible for, and what remains outside the trust kernel.

Northroot is a trust kernel for portable evidence.

It owns deterministic primitives:

  • canonical JSON
  • append-only records
  • content-derived event identity and hashes
  • receipts bound to evidence
  • offline verification
  • optional journal framing

It does not execute work, decide outcomes, store customer data, provide a live operating console, or encode client-specific workflow nouns.

Implementation Notes

The kernel stays useful because it refuses to become the runtime. Domain systems, reconciliation layers, operating consoles, and agent frameworks can build around it, but the core remains small enough to inspect.

Operational Lessons

When a concern requires interpretation, policy evaluation, or action, it belongs outside the kernel boundary.

Source

northroot/README.mdnorthroot/CORE_INVARIANTS.md

Explanations live here. Canonical contracts remain in source.
